folio.label: 144r
folio.l: IN Flaundres whilom , was a compaignye
Of ©onge folk , that haunteden folye
As ryot hasard , Stewes and Tauänes
Where as witú harpes , lutes and Gyternes
They daunce , and pleyen atte dees , bothe day & nygút
And ete also , and drynken ouer heröherö mygút
Thurgú whicú they doon , the deuele sacrifice
Witú Inne that deueles temple , in cursid wyse
By su¹fluitee , abhominable
Here othes ben so grete , and so dampnable
That it is grisly , for to here hem swere
Oure blissed lordes body , they to teere
Hem thougúte , that Iewes rent hi~ nougút I now
And ecú of hem , at otheres synne low
And rigút a noon , than comen Tombesterys
Fetise and smale , and ©onge Frutesterys
Syngeris witú harpes , Baudes , wafererys
Whicú ben , the verray deueles officeris
To kyndele and blowe , the fir of lecherie
That is annexed , vn to Glotonye
The holy wryt take I , to my wytnesse
That luxurye , is in wyn , and dronkenesse
¢ Lo how that dronken , Lotú , vnkendely
Lay by hise dougútres two , vn wetyngly
So dronke he was , he nyste what he wrougúte
Herodes , who so wel , the stories sougúte
Whan he of wyn , was replet at his feste
Rigút at his owne table , he ©af his heste
To slen the Baptist Ioún , ful giltelees
Senek seitú a good word , doutelees
He seitú , he can no difference fynde
Bitwix a man , that is out of his mynde
And a man , whicú that is dronkelewe
But that woodnesse , I fallen in a shrewe
Perseueretú lengere , than dotú dronkenesse
O Glotonye , ful of cursednesse
O cause first , of oure confusiou~
O original , of oure dampnaciou~
Til Crist had bougút vs , witú his blod a geyn
Lo how deere , shortly for to seyn
A bougút was , thilke cursed vilenye
Corrupt was al this world , for glotonye
Adam oure fader , and his wyf also
Fro Paradys , to labour and to woo
Were dryuen , for that xice , it is no drede
